meaning: Secretly, somewhat, a little bit
example: I lowkey love that song

meaning: Openly, definitely, without hiding it
example: I'm highkey obsessed with that show

meaning: No lie, for real, honestly
example: No cap, that was the best pizza ever

meaning: To do something really well, to be awesome or impressive
example: You totally slayed that presentation

meaning: A feeling, atmosphere, or energy someone or something gives off
example: This coffee shop has great vibes

meaning: To suddenly stop responding to someone's messages and cut off all communication
example: He ghosted her after three dates

spanish: tipo que hace cualquier cosa por alguien
meaning: Someone who does too much for a person they like, being overly submissive
example: Stop being a simp and set some boundaries

meaning: To show off or boast about something
example: He's flexing his new car on Instagram

meaning: Bitter, upset, or angry about something
example: Don't be salty just because you lost the game

meaning: Shocked, surprised, or very emotionally affected by something
example: I'm shook by that plot twist in the movie

meaning: Gossip or interesting information about someone
example: Tell me the tea! What happened between them?

meaning: Used to agree with someone or express that you relate to something
example: I don't want to work today. Mood.

meaning: Suspicious or questionable, something seems off
example: That story sounds sus to me

meaning: Okay, agreed, sounds good, I'll do it
example: You wanna grab lunch later? Bet, let's go

meaning: Really good, excellent, impressive, amazing
example: That new album is fire!

meaning: Exciting, excellent, amazing, or a great time
example: That party was lit!

meaning: Going to (future tense), informal contraction
example: I'm gonna go to the store later

meaning: Want to, informal contraction
example: Do you wanna go see a movie?

meaning: Have to, must, got to (informal)
example: I gotta finish this homework tonight

meaning: Kind of, somewhat, sort of
example: That movie was kinda boring

meaning: Sort of, kind of, somewhat
example: I'm sorta tired today

meaning: Let me, informal contraction
example: Lemme check my schedule real quick

meaning: Give me, informal contraction
example: Gimme a minute to get ready

meaning: You all, plural form of 'you' (especially Southern US)
example: Y'all should come visit us sometime

spanish: no soy, no estoy, no tiene
meaning: Am not, is not, are not, have not (very informal, non-standard)
example: I ain't got time for that

meaning: Don't know, informal contraction
example: What time is the movie? Dunno, let me check

meaning: Got you, I understand, agreed
example: I'll pick you up at 5pm. Gotcha!

meaning: Good, great, excellent, acceptable
example: That sounds cool to me

meaning: Excellent, fantastic, amazing
example: This pizza is awesome!

meaning: An unfortunate situation or disappointing event
example: You can't come? Aw, that's a bummer

meaning: To relax, calm down, or hang out casually
example: Let's just chill at home tonight

meaning: To spend time with someone casually, to socialize
example: Wanna hang out this weekend?

meaning: To become very worried, upset, or angry
example: Don't freak out, everything is going to be fine

meaning: To cheat or overcharge someone; an act of deception or theft
example: This restaurant is a total rip off

spanish: cometer un error, meter la pata
meaning: To make a mistake or do something wrong
example: I screwed up on that test

meaning: Did something perfectly or very well
example: You nailed it with that presentation!

meaning: Friend, buddy, used in British and Australian English
example: Cheers, mate! See you later

meaning: Thank you, goodbye, or a toast (British/Australian)
example: Cheers for your help!

meaning: Excellent, wonderful, fantastic (British)
example: That's a brilliant idea!

meaning: Terrible, awful, bad quality (British)
example: The weather has been rubbish all week

meaning: Very tired, exhausted (British)
example: I'm absolutely knackered after that workout

meaning: Suspicious, questionable, or of poor quality (British)
example: That deal sounds a bit dodgy to me

meaning: Playfully rude or disrespectful in a humorous way (British)
example: Don't be cheeky with me!

meaning: Very disappointed or upset (British)
example: I'm gutted that the match was cancelled

meaning: Arranged, solved, taken care of (British)
example: Don't worry, I've got it all sorted

meaning: To want to do something or feel like doing something (British)
example: Fancy a drink after work?

meaning: As soon as possible, abbreviation used in workplace communication
example: I need this report ASAP

meaning: For your information, used to share information in professional settings
example: FYI, the meeting has been moved to 3pm

meaning: A warning or advance notice about something
example: Heads up - there's going to be traffic on the highway

meaning: To check in or communicate with someone briefly
example: Let me touch base with you tomorrow

meaning: To return to a topic or issue at a later time
example: Let's circle back on this issue next week

meaning: Laugh out loud, used to indicate something is funny
example: That meme is hilarious LOL

meaning: Oh my God, used to express surprise or shock
example: OMG, you won't believe what just happened!

meaning: Be right back, indicates someone will return shortly
example: I need to grab some food, BRB

meaning: In my opinion, used to preface a personal viewpoint
example: IMO, that's not the best choice

spanish: para ser honesto, para decirte la verdad
meaning: To be honest, used when stating something truthfully or bluntly
example: TBH, I didn't really enjoy that movie
